title: Cons & Courtroom TV
subtitle: Long-con character studies, courtroom drama, twist-driven series.

items.title: The Sticky
items.pitch: Amazon. Margo Martindale leads a heist plot involving Quebec maple syrup reserves. Inspired by the real 2011-12 Quebec maple syrup heist.

critics.quote: Critical favorite of late 2024. Underseen. Tonally Coen-adjacent.

sections.name: II. High Priority — Long-Con Character Studies

items.title: Better Call Saul
items.whyPriority: Strongest single TV recommendation across this entire build alongside The Bureau. Slow-burn legal/con character study. Considered Breaking Bad's equal by the end. Your structural-rigor + emotional-weight pillars stack to maximum.
items.pitch: Jimmy McGill, struggling Albuquerque defense lawyer, slowly transforms into Saul Goodman. Then, after Breaking Bad, into Gene from Cinnabon.

critics.quote: 53 Emmy nominations across run. Final season widely considered Breaking Bad's equal.

items.title: The Night Of
items.whyPriority: HBO. John Turturro as a downtrodden NYC defense lawyer. Riz Ahmed as a Pakistani-American student accused of murder. Slow, devastating, courtroom-drama-as-character-piece.
items.pitch: A college student wakes up next to a dead woman with no memory of the night. The city's legal system processes him.

critics.quote: Won 5 Emmys including Lead Actor (Ahmed) and Supporting Actor (Camp).

items.title: Damages
items.dir: Daniel Zelman, Glenn Kessler, Todd A. Kessler
items.seasons: All 5 (ended 2012). FX for S1-3, DirecTV for S4-5.
items.whyPriority: Glenn Close vs. Rose Byrne. High-stakes civil litigation. Each season uses a flash-forward structure that rewards close attention.
items.pitch: A ruthless NYC litigator hires a young attorney. Each season has a high-stakes case and a hidden game.

critics.quote: Close won two Lead Actress Emmys. Considered one of the great prestige legal dramas.

items.title: Goliath
items.whyPriority: Amazon. Billy Bob Thornton as a washed-up lawyer taking on impossible cases. David E. Kelley (LA Law, The Practice, Big Little Lies) at his sharpest in years.
items.pitch: A disgraced ex-partner of a powerful LA firm builds his cases out of a beachside dive bar. Each season takes on a different titan of industry.

critics.quote: Thornton won the Globe for the role. Critically warm; underseen relative to its quality.

sections.name: III. High Priority — Heist & Con Series

items.title: Hustle
items.whyPriority: Per your selection. BBC. London grifters running long cons against deserving marks. Procedurally elegant. The British heist-show standard.
items.pitch: A team of grifters runs elaborate long cons in London. The con-of-the-week structure escalates across seasons.

critics.quote: BBC ratings hit. Considered the model for the modern British heist procedural.

items.title: Sneaky Pete
items.whyPriority: Per your selection. Amazon. Giovanni Ribisi as a con man assuming his cellmate's identity. Bryan Cranston produced and recurring.
items.pitch: A grifter on the run from a New York gangster takes the identity of his prison cellmate and moves in with the family.

critics.quote: Critical favorite. Cancelled before its time. Cult devotion.

items.title: Lupin
items.whyPriority: Netflix's biggest non-English breakout. Omar Sy as a gentleman thief modeled on Maurice Leblanc's Arsène Lupin character.
items.pitch: A modern Parisian gentleman thief carries out cons inspired by his late father's favorite character: Arsène Lupin.

critics.quote: Netflix's most-watched non-English series at launch. Sy's performance carries it.

items.title: Money Heist (La Casa de Papel)
items.pitch: Spanish heist series. The Professor plans to print his own money inside the Royal Mint. The show's structural construction of the heist is the appeal.

critics.quote: Most-watched non-English Netflix series in history at launch.

sections.name: IV. Medium Priority
sections.desc: Strong probable fits in adjacent territory.

items.title: Presumed Innocent (2024)
items.seasons: Limited series; S2 announced as anthology
items.whyPriority: Apple TV+. Adaptation of the 1990 Pakula film of the same name. Jake Gyllenhaal as a prosecutor accused of murdering his colleague. Different ending than the film.
items.pitch: A Chicago prosecutor is accused of murdering a colleague with whom he had an affair. He must defend himself.

items.title: Anatomy of a Scandal
items.dir: David E. Kelley & Melissa James Gibson
items.pitch: Netflix. British political thriller. Sienna Miller, Rupert Friend. A junior Conservative minister is accused of rape. The trial unfolds.

critics.quote: Mixed-positive critical reception; strong audience response.

items.title: The Defenders
items.whyPriority: The seminal courtroom drama of American TV. Reginald Rose (12 Angry Men) created it. Father-son defense team takes on civil rights, abortion, McCarthyism, capital punishment. Unimaginable today for being so progressive then.
items.pitch: A Manhattan father-son defense team takes on the most controversial cases of their era.

critics.quote: Won three consecutive Outstanding Drama Emmys (1962-64). Considered the prestige TV courtroom drama template.

items.title: Boston Legal
items.whyPriority: Spader and Shatner at their best. Kelley's most playful courtroom show. Tonally distinct: comedy-drama hybrid with sharp political and legal arguments.
items.pitch: Two ethically loose attorneys at a Boston firm take on cases too strange for the senior partners.

critics.quote: Spader won three consecutive Lead Actor in a Drama Emmys.

items.title: The Good Wife
items.whyPriority: Network legal drama with prestige-show ambition. Julianna Margulies as a politician's wife who returns to law after his scandal. Sharper than its CBS pedigree suggests.
items.pitch: Alicia Florrick returns to legal practice after her politician husband's sex-and-corruption scandal lands him in prison.

critics.quote: Critical favorite throughout run. Margulies won two Lead Actress Emmys.

items.title: The Lincoln Lawyer (TV)
items.pitch: Netflix. Manuel Garcia-Rulfo as Mickey Haller, the LA lawyer who works out of his Lincoln Town Car. Connelly novel adaptations.

critics.quote: Netflix workhorse. Better than its category suggests.

items.title: Murder One
items.seasons: S1 follows one case across all 23 episodes
items.pitch: Steven Bochco's experiment: one murder case unfolds across an entire 23-episode season. Daniel Benzali anchors S1.

critics.quote: Considered ahead of its time; structurally pioneered the season-long-arc legal drama.

items.title: How to Get Away with Murder
items.pitch: Viola Davis as a defense attorney and law professor. Davis won the Lead Actress Emmy in 2015 — the first Black woman ever to do so.

critics.quote: Davis Emmy historic. Show's later seasons declined.

items.title: Bull (UK 2014, not US)
items.pitch: Note: The UK 'The Bridge' / 'The Bridge of Spies' / etc. and other titles confuse easily. The US 'Bull' (2016, with Michael Weatherly) is generally not recommended despite long run; quality issues + behind-scenes problems.
